seoTitle: How to Send a Fax in Outlook Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Send a fax straight from Outlook using an email-to-fax gateway provider, without a physical fax machine or scanning documents by hand.
intro: You don't need a physical fax machine to send documents anymore. By pairing Outlook's regular email composer with an email-to-fax gateway service, you can send a PDF or Word document to any fax number directly from your inbox. This guide covers formatting the recipient address, attaching your document, and confirming the transmission went through.

steps.heading: Choose a fax gateway provider
steps.body: Sign up with a reputable email-to-fax service, since Outlook itself doesn't fax anything without one bridging the connection to a fax network.

steps.heading: Compose a new email
steps.body: Open Outlook and start a new message just as you would for a normal email.

steps.heading: Format the recipient address
steps.body: Enter the destination fax number combined with your provider's specific domain extension in the To field, following the exact syntax your gateway requires.

steps.heading: Write your cover text and attach the document
steps.body: Type your message body, which the gateway will typically convert into a fax cover sheet, then attach your PDF or Word file to be transmitted as the fax pages.

steps.heading: Send and verify the transmission
steps.body: Send the email and watch for an automated confirmation receipt from your provider that tells you whether the fax was delivered successfully.

faq.question: Can I send a fax from Outlook without a fax machine?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you use an email-to-fax gateway provider, Outlook can send the fax entirely through your normal email composer.

faq.question: What file types can I fax through this method?
faq.answer: Most gateway providers accept PDF, DOCX, or TIFF attachments, which get converted into fax-compatible pages.

faq.question: How do I know if my fax was actually delivered?
faq.answer: Your gateway provider sends an automated email confirmation receipt after transmission, which you should check to verify successful delivery.

faq.question: Is this method secure for sensitive documents?
faq.answer: Reputable gateway providers offer encryption, but you should confirm your provider's security settings before sending confidential legal or financial paperwork.
